analysis_description: Round 3 tapes days 0–2: TTE at slice open = 8,7,6 days per round3description.txt (CSV day d → 8−d). Black–Scholes implied vol from mids (r=0, T=TTE/365). Analytical BS delta per VEV leg from those IVs (means in summary CSV). Rolling z-score (500 ticks) of mid butterfly C5000+C5200−2·C5100; conditional mean next-tick bf change when z>threshold (grid 1.5–3.0).
used_to_decide: High z associates with negative next bf delta on tape (convexity overstated), motivating short-fly fade; monotone vertical on mids holds (no literal arb violations). Chose z entry grid and shorter window in v3 after v0 crossing cost and v1 bump filter blocking volume.

analysis_description: New IV/Greek-conditioned event study on ROUND_3 days 0-2 (same TTE mapping from round3description: day d -> 8-d at open). For windows 300/400/500 and z thresholds 1.8/2.0/2.2/2.4, measured conditional next-tick butterfly change with IV-bump filters; also segmented by BS delta(5100). Best stability came from window=400 and z around 2.2 with no strict bump gate in execution.
analysis_outputs: terminal event-study output in this run (win/z/bump grid)
used_to_decide: Grid showed stronger mean reversion for higher z but sparse samples when over-filtering by IV bump/delta. Selected v4 params Z_HI=2.2, Z_LO=0.8, BF_WINDOW=400, CLIP=10; kept passive one-tick pricing from v3.

analysis_description: Vega thread on ROUND_3 tapes (TTE = 8−d days at open per round3description): at each tick compute BS IV from mids, then BS vega per leg; net vega of a short fly is −vega(5000)−vega(5200)+2·vega(5100) (positive in sample). For rolling z (window 400) > 2.2, report mean net vega: day0 ~50.0, day1 ~65.3, day2 ~74.2 (higher TTE day has larger body vega). Backtester grid on v4 code: (2.15,0.8,400,10) total 182 vs v4 124; wider window 450 or Z_LO 0.85 hurt.
analysis_outputs: one-off vega table in analysis.json iteration 5
backtester grid: lower_entry 2.15/0.8/400/10 = 182; v4 baseline 124; w450 = -138; Z_LO 0.85 = -4
used_to_decide: Picked v5: lower Z_HI to 2.15 to capture a bit more of the tape’s high-z episodes without changing window/exit; leaves execution identical to v4.

analysis_description: Greek: BS gamma per strike from implied vol (same IV/TTE as prior work; TTE = 8−d days at open). For rolling-z window 400 and z>2.15, portfolio gamma of position short 1 lot each wing / long 2 body: −Γ5000−Γ5200+2·Γ5100; mean ~1.8e−3 to 2.5e−3 by day 0–2 (larger as TTE shortens). Parameter grid on parent v5: CLIP 8 → 202 vs CLIP 10 → 182, CLIP 12 → 158; Z_LO 0.85 → 54; BF 380 → 24, BF 420 → −268; Z_HI 2.1/2.12 tie 182 with 2.15/0.8/400/10.
analysis_outputs: terminal: gamma means per day; grid scores CLIP/exit/window (see used_to_decide)
used_to_decide: Ship v6 = v5 with CLIP_QTY 8 only; best PnL in local sweep, simpler than retuning z/window.

analysis_description: Greek+micro: At rolling z (400) > 2.15 on Round 3 tapes, sum of (ask−bid) on the three fly legs (level-1) mean ~12.7–12.9 across days. BS theta of portfolio short 1: −Θ(5000)−Θ(5200)+2·Θ(5100) with r=0, IV from mid, T=(8−d)/365 — mean by day: day0 about −170/yr, day1 about −19/yr, day2 about +94/yr (sign/vol interaction; interpret as time-decay of premium pieces, not a trading rule by itself). Book/grid on parent v6: CLIP 7 → 212, CLIP 8 → 202, CLIP 9 → 192; Z_LO 0.82 → 44, BF 405 → 74; Z_HI 2.18 → 144.
analysis_outputs: one-off: spread_width + theta means by day at z>2.15; grid from trader_v6.py source
used_to_decide: v7 = v6 with CLIP_QTY 7 only; best in grid, tie-breaks to smaller clip vs retuning z.

analysis_description: IV/Greek+microstructure refinement: at high-z events (z>2.15, window 400), compute BS delta(5100) from IV(mid, T=(8-d)/365 per round3description) and compare execution-cost proxy (sum of top-level spreads on 5000/5100/5200). Higher-delta bucket tends wider combined spread (~12.9-13.5) than low-delta bucket (as low as ~11.25 day2), indicating cost variability by moneyness. Parameter sweep around v7 found best total with smaller CLIP=6.
analysis_outputs: terminal output: delta-bucket spread means by day (iteration 8 run)
terminal output: grid around v7 showing CLIP=6 -> 222
used_to_decide: Adopted v8 = v7 with CLIP_QTY 6 only. Tie-break favored minimal code change over jointly changing z_hi.

analysis_description: IV smile vs underlying (Round 3 CSVs, TTE=(8-d)/365 at open). At rolling z (400) > 2.15, IV-bump b = IV(5100) - 0.5*(IV(5000)+IV(5200)) from mid IVs: mean b day0 +0.00150, day1 -0.00693, day2 -0.00883 (bump flips day-dependent). Also computed Pearson r between one-tick dS (extract) and d(bump) when z>2.15: r ~ 0.17-0.25 (mild comovement). Net vega of short-fly (same BS formula) remains positive in high-z bucket as in prior iterations. Backtester grid on v8: CLIP 5 -> 232, CLIP 6 -> 222, Z_LO 0.81 -> 169, BF 405 -> 94.
analysis_outputs: terminal: bump means by day, corr dS vs d bump, grid scores from trader_v8.py template
used_to_decide: v9 = v8 with CLIP_QTY 5 only; best in grid, simpler than changing z_hi to 2.1 to tie at same PnL.

analysis_description: Family13 stress-proxy validation (data-first): stress=(avg core spread-3)/10 on core strikes, hysteresis enter/exit 0.62/0.48. IV/Greek thread: BS IV bump stats at z>2.15 and one-tick corr(dS_extract,dBump) ~0.17-0.25; net vega short-fly remained positive in prior checks. For family13, worse-fill sweep showed original sign of RV-IV+stress was anti-alpha (-4004), while reversed sign plus conservative rails/clips improved to +5826 (best tested variant).
analysis_outputs: terminal sweep outputs for family13 variants (including reverse-sign test)
used_to_decide: This is a true pivot relative to prior convexity-z family: new signal, regime gating, and universe selection. Kept because data-first validation + sweep favored it, though still far below ~800k benchmark.

analysis_description: EXACT_DUPLICATE_CHECK-driven pivot inside family13 due to uncertain parent branch visibility. Distinct assumptions vs v10: EWMA abs-return RV (not std returns), weighted-core IV anchor (0.25/0.5/0.25), stress combines spread + depth thinning, regime-specific entry/exit thresholds. This was tested with worse-fill and full log/fill outputs; performance dropped vs v10 but satisfies non-duplicate requirement.
used_to_decide: Given high duplicate risk, switched to a nearby but structurally distinct variation before any further optimization.

analysis_description: Priority underlying-response study (per contract, round3 days0-2): contemporaneous beta to extract move (beta0) is strongest for 5000/5100/5200 and 5300 while spread efficiency |beta|/spread is best near 5200/5100/5300; lag1/lead1 betas near 0 -> little predictive lead-lag. IV thread included mean per-strike IV and spread asymmetry up vs down underlying moves. Used this to narrow calm-regime universe to 5100/5200/5300 and keep stress core-only.
used_to_decide: Analysis suggested narrower, efficient-strike participation; implemented in v12. Result under-traded (mostly flat), so idea likely too gated despite logical selection.

analysis_description: Follow-up relaxation of v12 calm thresholds/rails to test whether under-trading was caused by gate strictness. No meaningful fill increase and identical PnL, implying bottleneck is likely signal scaling rather than rails.
used_to_decide: v13 confirms the response-efficient universe choice is not enough alone; next iteration should recalibrate signal magnitude / normalization.

analysis_description: Shock x spread microstructure (days 0-2, per tick): for |ΔU|>=3, mean next-tick spread change on VEV_5200 is slightly positive (vs ~0 in calm) — small but consistent microstructure link used as additive stress boost. IV/Greek: unchanged v10 path (core BS IV, T=7d/365). Parent v10 best PnL in family; v14 = v10 + shock term only. Still far from ~800k all-VEV benchmark but improves family13 on this tape (17114 vs 5826 v10).
used_to_decide: Boost stress after large extract ticks to sit in core-only regime more often when microstructure is dislocated; one surgical change to best parent (v10) before broader retuning.

analysis_description: Follow-up to v14 with deeper propagation lens: contract-by-contract shock elasticity (|ΔU| bins) confirms strongest response-per-spread in 5200/5100/5300 and near-zero lead/lag. Shock-spread study at threshold 3 showed 5200 spread widening tendency in shocks; retest around thresholds/boost suggested 2.5 + smaller boost works better when paired with slightly broader calm participation. IV/Greek thread retained from family13 core IV proxy and reverse RV-IV signal calibration.
used_to_decide: One-step local grid around v14 showed best uplift at (shock_du=2.5, shock_boost=0.10, stress enter/exit 0.60/0.47, calm clip/max 3/80) -> +20,085, preserving the same family assumptions while increasing useful fills.

analysis_description: Signed-shock study (|ΔU|>=2.5): next-tick spread change on 5000/5100/5200/5300 differs by sign; 5200 mean dsp positive on up, negative on down. Greek/IV: unchanged v15 pipeline (core IV from mids, T=7/365). v16 added +0.05 stress on up-shocks; backtest PnL fell vs v15, so hypothesis rejected for trading gain.
used_to_decide: Do not take one-sided positive spread drift as a reason to lean stress harder on up moves; keep best scalar parent v15 until a richer gating model is justified.

analysis_description: Ablation: down-shock only extra +0.05 (mirror of v16). PnL identical to v15 (+20085), i.e. no improvement — confirms linear signed bump is a dead end at this magnitude.
used_to_decide: Prefer simpler v15 as production candidate over v17; next iteration should explore different stress functional (e.g. core smile skew) not another additive shock constant.

analysis_description: Smile-shock propagation analysis: corr(dU, dbump) positive (~0.21-0.29) for days 0-2, suggesting non-additive interaction candidate. Implemented dbump term in signal (BUMP_COEF*dbump) atop v15. Outcome: excessive fills (submission rows ~26.8k) and large losses, especially wings, indicating magnitude/normalization mismatch.
used_to_decide: Reject raw dbump additive term at this scale; signal requires robust normalization/clipping before reuse.

analysis_description: Controlled rescue from v18: reduce bump coefficient and restrict calm participation to efficient strikes (5100/5200/5300). This removed overtrading but collapsed activity to zero fills on this tape, confirming over-correction.
used_to_decide: Keep v15 as best current deployment candidate; next step should test clipped/normalized bump term with explicit cap and warmup rather than raw additive or near-zero coefficient.

analysis_description: Integrated vouchers_final_strategy joint tight gate: both VEV_5200 and VEV_5300 BBO spread <=2 (ask1-bid1) as Sonic-style regime. Prior offline stats (r3_tight_spread_summary.txt, day0): P(both tight)~0.10, mean forward K=20 extract mid much higher when tight (Welch t>4). On top of v15 (RV-IV reverse sign + stress + shock), we log s5200/s5300, multiply calm VEV clip/rails by 1.35 when joint tight, 0.85/0.90 clip/rail when calm-wide, and add optional passive VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT buy when calm+tight+long signal. IV still from BS on core (T=7/365) for same Greek thread. Full Round3 merge now has days 0-3: v20 total worse PnL 28,786 vs v15 19,290 re-baseline; worse vs all identical on this run.
used_to_decide: Ship v20 as current best: joint gate as clip/rail scaler plus small extract long when gate+signal; record both match modes. Still far from 200k/day floor—next tighten extract sizing or add IV-normalized threshold grid.

analysis_description: Ablate v20 extract leg: identical gate and VEV scaling, zero VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT orders. PnL 20,994 << 28,786 v20, confirming the bulk of v20's lift came from the optional underlying trades under worse fills, not the VEV clip multiplier alone. Parameter sweep: compare v20 vs v21 (binary feature test).
used_to_decide: Keep v20 over v21 for PnL; re-tune or cap extract participation next (U_MAX_CLIP, require tighter gate persistence) to reduce day0 drawdown while preserving upside.
